refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR specifies a single obligation: ensuring teleoperation communication quality meets a defined packet loss limit. It is focused on what the system must achieve (support teleoperation with specified communication quality) without specifying how to achieve it, thus it is solution-neutral and atomic.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation.
The NGHE solution shall ensure communication quality meets the customer-defined packet loss limit during teleoperation.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission to maintain packet loss within limits
Design a dedicated communication channel with guaranteed quality of service (QoS) parameters to meet packet loss requirements
Incorporate real-time monitoring and dynamic adjustment of communication parameters to ensure packet loss stays below threshold
Use buffering and smoothing techniques at the receiver to compensate for packet loss and maintain teleoperation quality
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission to maintain packet loss within limits

potential_sources: Dependency on network conditions and error correction algorithms
Interaction between retransmission logic and teleoperation control timing
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates the communication quality control within the protocol layer, supporting independence from other system functions. However, care must be taken to ensure retransmission mechanisms do not introduce unacceptable latency affecting teleoperation responsiveness.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions remain within agreed parameters; unexpected degradation may cause packet loss to exceed limits
Adaptive error correction and retransmission may increase latency, potentially impacting teleoperation performance
Worksite safety policies may impose constraints on communication methods or retransmission frequency
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including latency constraints alongside packet loss in the FR to ensure the DP balances error correction with teleoperation responsiveness.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the network communication stack and protocol management components responsible for handling data transmission, including adaptive error correction and retransmission mechanisms.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional interface for sending and receiving data packets over the network, essential for implementing communication protocols.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Outputs status and performance metrics related to communication quality and error correction effectiveness.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as the DP directly relates to communication protocol implementation; minimal diffusion since error correction and retransmission are core to this module.
Contains some communication components but primarily focused on vehicle control; implementing DP here would cause significant diffusion to other modules.
Uses communication protocols but focuses on remote control logic; DP implementation here would spread communication concerns across modules.
Safety Services may monitor communication quality but is not responsible for protocol implementation; poor containment and high diffusion risk.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for implementing the adaptive communication protocol, as it centralizes communication logic and minimizes diffusion across the system.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on lower-level hardware interfaces not fully encapsulated within NGHE Solution.
Changes in communication standards may require updates across multiple modules if interfaces are not well defined.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If containment risks materialize, consider refining the DP to explicitly separate protocol logic from hardware interface management, enabling clearer modular boundaries and reducing diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by specifying an adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission to maintain packet loss within customer-defined limits, aligning well with the acceptance criteria of packet loss ≤ 1%.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on lower-level hardware interfaces not fully encapsulated within NGHE Solution module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating that the DP is well encapsulated within this IM boundary. Other modules have lower containment and higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the implementation boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring packet loss metrics output via the Telemetry interface, which reports communication quality and error correction effectiveness, enabling validation against the acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and well justified, with explicit interfaces defined for network communication and telemetry outputs supporting traceability from FR to IM.

recommended_revisions: Monitor and manage dependencies on lower-level hardware interfaces to ensure they are well encapsulated within the NGHE Solution module to avoid diffusion risks.
Consider refining the DP to separate protocol logic from hardware interface management if containment risks arise, enabling clearer modular boundaries and reducing diffusion across modules.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification and verification feasibility. The DP directly addresses the FR with an adaptive communication protocol meeting the acceptance criteria, and the DP is well encapsulated within the NGHE Solution IM with high containment and low diffusion scores. Verification methods are clearly defined and traceability is established. Although there are some diffusion risks related to lower-level hardware interfaces, these are manageable through monitoring and potential refinement of DP, which are noted as recommended revisions rather than blockers. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to maintain the current level and focus on managing identified risks rather than decomposing further at this stage.

revise.why: To separate protocol logic from hardware interface management if diffusion risks increase, improving modular boundaries and reducing diffusion across modules.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module sufficiently encapsulates the DP with minimal diffusion.
Verification via telemetry interface is reliable and comprehensive.
Dependencies on lower-level hardware interfaces can be effectively managed within the current IM.
open_risks: Potential dependency on lower-level hardware interfaces not fully encapsulated within NGHE Solution module.
Diffusion risks if hardware interface management is not properly contained.
evidence_needed: Monitoring data on hardware interface dependencies and their encapsulation.
Verification results from telemetry interface showing packet loss metrics meeting acceptance criteria.
